Peter -- What were you doing two weekends ago? If you're one of the Koch brothers or a member of their $100,000-and-up donor network, you were at a lavish three-day retreat in California, where you announced your top political targets for 2018.

No one from our team was there (and we're guessing neither were you), but we still got the news anyway: **Joe Donnelly isn 't just on the Kochs' list for 2018 defeat -- he's one of their four top targets!**

It's not surprising. After Joe stood up to big special interests on taxes and health care, we knew they'd make him a top target. But now that it's official, the first major wave of attacks is coming _any day_. **And those attacks threaten to DESTROY the grassroots momentum we 've built when they hammer the airwaves with lies about Joe's record.**

There's only one way to fight back: by hitting our $10,000 **Media Response Fund** goal by midnight tomorrow. **Will you chip in?**

---
| | | Feb. 11, 2018
---
| Hope you had a great weekend!

Leah's been busy talking to grassroots conservatives across the state. This week on her Wisconsin Way tour, she visited Marathon, Portage, Rock, Ozaukee, Chippewa and Barron Counties. This weekend alone, Leah and her team hit 11 Republican Lincoln Day Dinners and caucuses all over Wisconsin.
---
|
---
---
| As Leah's been traveling the state, she's been hearing from workers from Rice Lake to Mequon who are seeing more money in their pay check due to tax reform.

Committed to further tax relief, Leah signed the Americans for Tax Reform pledge. She vows to oppose any new taxes or tax increases as a U.S. Senator.
---
|
---
| On Thursday, students from college campuses across Wisconsin formed a Students Choose Leah coalition with [more than 140 members](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=1fff8e3a208449adbca519696de3292b&_e=CWfzz_MM3lvyEGbu3w7iF0j2dWN0h7hGRIiDRGJJ3m42ps14q5VKrPRx-Zt9sUxWi3bQXQiGSjRwijVPk-7pzACHSF-3l8RtuiYDImRu1yAgdlhIqQr6dCGXsTd0eIW2De8S48HuuKheC5t71SFME_3j8Gm1tV392JOH64XJE6avZ_-Ed8L91see5tk9mTCxltug2Br9A7BPeYHWMo5DcN20TuGJRu3RBs3qvF32E3xfSFun43ShaJLVLAVERzFiAraltWq9q1hfIV9fHvHlCzjEku4pEG4LKLbPJ3wmfb2Tn4Aumcl4vEp3tYPWnDFGrGKEmXtvv7E8Xzx0ok1FsQ%3D%3D) already.
---
|
---
| “Students know Leah is someone they can trust to represent the values they care about,” said Alesha Guenther, Students Choose Leah co-chair from the UW–Madison.

Ryan Ring, Students Choose Leah co-chair from the University of Wisconsin–Eau Claire said: "From supporting School Choice to the tuition freeze, she has consistently stood up for students. I am proud to stand with Leah as she runs against Tammy Baldwin."
---
|
---
| Leah also kept busy with legislative duties. On Tuesday she held a Health Committee Hearing, where members unanimously passed the Right to Try bill.

And on Wednesday and Thursday, she worked with her colleagues in Joint Finance to approve a bill she authored to give communities more resources to curb drug trafficking in the fight against heroin abuse.
---
|
---
---
| Please keep up with Leah's travels across the state as she runs for the Senate by following her [Facebook](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=1fff8e3a208449adbca519696de3292b&_e=Fpcd29Mt0FJmyy2nAAizhaDZUTdrWMFc4kGrsBqJkredmxQgaUtD72YtAGBBqAR_7vLf3jACHuKJ0hvLgypTC06_8R_amu4I2zQCk41hM88tAWP0mIGZMhcigQFfljvHpIfagiHtA3X2423gGQmn8KNAw2_2uiwC8Tzeq1i6xvHknxN_tmzfSygOJAA6FMufy9N5gRokxIajW-UHc0nE7z5W7OrfXLQRRMZ2FiF8SZlm-VD6ruYA4xDATlf2oxQtSziYlH994vRgommRhoW6zw%3D%3D), [Twitter](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=1fff8e3a208449adbca519696de3292b&_e=G9fdyUbuaq1uMgLEZ4dD4_kc-GskBf3lgckJSQum8i19xLYOorUWNE8rbhPdG6vRDkq-59fvUi5a5TDQFQDEHqjw0J_UF8QLpOA3ueWhtpaEt8gjbHYadinATg-r0lI2jZHQKjg0XS3JJXZjW95Mjn4hilSaEI4B6WZmbpbzt_VP7J-jIT_GA4pv2DM0q1oxeve9yLiW_t7cW6_uEUCOP5K1H6dKfArFXvqV9wKAyxrbuPpsYBrlQ-bm3phPu8meRZc5LezlRRpnDbsPMl2dhg%3D%3D) and [Instagram](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=1fff8e3a208449adbca519696de3292b&_e=rKgjuO3ZrWqZ51LcwTHKcqP3QWJ9rsFFu3qIlWfY7XoynC0S7iBsDVKZ4q3pk0nAz4b-2YZ91mqthMP3l5jvtWIZE_nKuQUqD3RDeFd2lFdAl2hIt2j5Tng75Z9ZmnHYyNq_fTjS1lK2Mt-hpltZ_i7iBXi3cQ69d1Mfe33S9qA-QkIB5MDEvJiPXcGI7xy5MRsGjQL1w17OnzZOnMdxFRALapkvND_K48yASfD8z6iVXkbJMwYPP9FsMRlKMs8kEZOyhSYkW8KeMnsEMkXcZQ%3D%3D) pages.

name: The White House
subject: The President’s plan to rebuild America
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b644c4112304e6801f1600078982086bf38148a033a4417ffa0974d2e3343b3f55077a4eeae852a7c3e51a441624c17b47c61825b50266e4

JUST RELEASED: In President Donald J. Trump’s latest Weekly Address, he discusses his meeting with law enforcement leaders last week on the scourge of violence from the gang MS-13. “Glaring loopholes in our laws have allowed criminals and gang members to break into our country,” President Trump says.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b644c4112304e6802f919998cde6c771a7c35ce2e0ab1f2ef04935749d1c390a3e8bb4cf7a6fcc3d42e3b04750037e139c340585c158b0c9
Watch his Address here.

-------------------

The President’s plan to rebuild America

America has long been a Nation of builders, President Trump told the country January 30 in his first State of the Union Address. “We built the Empire State Building in just 1 year—is it not a disgrace that it can now take 10 years just to get a permit approved for a simple road?”

He called on leaders of both parties in Congress to come together to deliver the safe, reliable, and modern infrastructure that Americans deserve. Today, he will outline his legislative principles to accomplish just that:

-
$200 billion in Federal funds to spur at least $1.5 trillion in infrastructure investments with partners at the state, local, tribal, and private level
- New investments in rural America, which has been left behind for too long
- Decision-making authority will return to state and local governments
- Regulatory barriers that needlessly get in the way of infrastructure projects will be removed
- Permitting for infrastructure projects will be streamlined and shortened
- America’s workforce will be supported and strengthened

“We will build gleaming new roads, bridges, highways, railways, and waterways all across our land,” President Trump said. “And we will do it with American heart, and American hands, and American grit.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b644c4112304e68028778220cd9fa45bef992ca4b4d5d667fa55bc025cfc3ba50187bc775042e370ea9360d2b278aaf2342733f005984677
Read more about how President Trump plans to rebuild America’s infrastructure.

To save Gaza, turn on the lights

The Kerem Shalom border crossing between Israel and the Gaza Strip has long been a flashpoint for conflict. Last weekend, Israeli authorities foiled a Hamas attempt to smuggle explosives at the crossing into Gaza—including components expected to be used to launch projectiles into Israel.

While Hamas’ attacks on Israel are well-documented, Hamas’ exploitation of the people it claims to govern is appalling in its own right. Hamas can’t even keep the lights on, and must depend on the generosity of the Palestinian Authority for a few hours of power each day.

“Imagine what the people of Gaza could do with the $100 million Iran has historically given Hamas and other Palestinian terrorist groups annually that Hamas uses for weapons and tunnels to attack Israel,” wrote Jason Greenblatt, Assistant to President Donald J. Trump and Special Representative for International Negotiations.

Read more:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b644c4112304e680bcd3c829d183b336ee1c458bba99e6f93d0157eecb03801a6661d7745094a561aeb379d433956eec86055716e898a160
To Save Gaza, Turn on the Lights

A $10,000 check

The afternoon of President Trump’s first State of the Union Address 13 days ago, The Washington Post ran a story about a special interaction the President had with campaign volunteer Shane Bouvet:

The unusual meeting was arranged after Trump said he read a profile about Bouvet in The Washington Post. The struggling single father had spent nights working and days volunteering for Trump, who Bouvet hoped could bring renewal to his home town of Stonington, Ill., and other corners hard hit by the decline in U.S. industry.

On Monday, [a $10,000 check from the President] arrived in the mail.

... Bouvet said he planned to use the money to pay for chemotherapy for his father, who he said is suffering from bladder cancer.

On Friday, President Trump once again met with Bouvet—along with his father, Donald Bouvet, who is now cancer-free. “And I just think it’s an incredible story. Very rarely do I hear a story like this,” the President said.

“I want to thank Mr. President for everything and the blessings that he’s done for me and my family,” Shane Bouvet said.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b644c4112304e6802265302cc81359030c7c12ccda166def43d80524df7a6b22f2b1d1095f3d9cc008f1f5b1e20d0a60e8810a736cc42614
Read more about President Trump’s powerful encounter with the Bouvet family.

Photo of the Day

Vice President and Mrs. Karen Pence at the 2018 Winter Olympics | February 9, 2018 (Official White House Photo by D. Myles Cullen)

POTUS TODAY

Today, after hosting a meeting with state and local officials about his infrastructure initiative, President Trump will meet with Vice President Mike Pence. Later, the President will have lunch with the Vice President and Secretary of Education Betsy DeVos.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b644c4112304e680e740d46c7975ccb2c3fcf4ca6e3830c19092a339ac792bdc56c9fe7553eff505e32ebef1576b73acba8ed675de67d16c
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b644c4112304e680f317094dc675f41639d21d77bfa51f898619d9964f426117f5681223a4453d79abbd7e6afc056b8fb4ae7504f0bd3ce9
Contact the White House |

name: DNC HQ
subject: We can win a House seat before November, Derek:
body:
On March 13th, there is an important special election in Pennsylvania -- and Conor Lamb needs your help to win.
---
Derek --

I'd like to talk to you about Conor Lamb -- the Democratic candidate in the upcoming special election in Pennsylvania's 18th District.

Conor is a 33-year-old Marine and former federal prosecutor, and he's currently fighting to flip a critical House seat from red to blue. He has refused to accept corporate PAC donations, so he's relying on grassroots supporters to fund his campaign.

The special election is March 13th, and these final weeks will be the difference between winning and losing. So I am asking you today:

**Will you split a $10 donation between Conor Lamb and the DNC? Your support will send Conor to the House and elect Democrats in all 50 states this year.**

**[ DONATE: $10](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522166/4067b0a/5527c5346/7e52a842/3392795159/VEsEAA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/conor-lamb?amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=25+-+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20180212_abl_dnc&source=em_20180212_abl_dnc&refcode=em_20180212_abl_dnc")
[DONATE: $25](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522166/4067b0a/5527c5346/7e52a843/3392795159/VEsEAw/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/conor-lamb?amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=26+-+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20180212_abl_dnc&source=em_20180212_abl_dnc&refcode=em_20180212_abl_dnc")
[DONATE: $50](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522166/4067b0a/5527c5346/7e52a841/3392795159/VEsEAg/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/conor-lamb?amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=27+-+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20180212_abl_dnc&source=em_20180212_abl_dnc&refcode=em_20180212_abl_dnc")
[DONATE: $100](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522166/4067b0a/5527c5346/7e52a84e/3392795159/VEsEDQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/conor-lamb?amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=28+-+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20180212_abl_dnc&source=em_20180212_abl_dnc&refcode=em_20180212_abl_dnc")
[DONATE: $200](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522166/4067b0a/5527c5346/7e52a84c/3392795159/VEsEDA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/conor-lamb?amount=200&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=29+-+DONATE+200&utm_campaign=em_20180212_abl_dnc&source=em_20180212_abl_dnc&refcode=em_20180212_abl_dnc")
[Or donate another amount.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522166/4067b0a/5527c5346/7e52a84a/3392795159/VEsFBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/conor-lamb?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=30+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20180212_abl_dnc&source=em_20180212_abl_dnc&refcode=em_20180212_abl_dnc")
** Let me tell you, Derek -- flipping this seat would send a clear message to the GOP that Democrats can win anywhere we fight. It would send shockwaves through the Republican Party that every district is up for grabs.

We won't take back the House in November unless we compete for every seat -- and that starts right now, with the upcoming special election in Pennsylvania.

[**Chip in $10 today to split a donation between Conor Lamb and the DNC.**](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522166/4067b0a/5527c5346/7e52a848/3392795159/VEsFBA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/conor-lamb?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=31+-+Chip+in++if+consinConsGroup28497++set+ma&utm_campaign=em_20180212_abl_dnc&source=em_20180212_abl_dnc&refcode=em_20180212_abl_dnc")

If enough people step up to support Conor ahead of the special election, I know he can win.

Thanks for all you do,

Amanda

Amanda Brown Lierman
Political and Organizing Director
Democratic National Committee

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: Pruitt celebrates one year at the EPA
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | | | "Newly released emails show Environmental Protection Agency Administrator Scott Pruitt personally monitored efforts last year to excise much of the information about climate change from the agency's website" _\-- Associated Press_ ; 2/2/2018
---
This month marks one year since Scott Pruitt took charge of the EPA.
What's he done, so far? He's slowly and systematically removed mentions of climate change from his agency's website -- _"especially President Obama 's signature effort to reduce planet-warming carbon emissions from coal-fired power plants."_
[_**Demand Pruitt and the Trump administration stop denying the facts of climate change and start doing something about it.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f81/622b7b5b/194eefb3/307bb5f8/2258114840/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180212")
President Trump and Scott Pruitt have both denied climate change. Maybe they think that somehow, removing the term from the Internet will stop the atmosphere from warming?
**Florida families are at risk from a changing climate.** Our sea levels are rising. Our low-elevation areas are threatened by ever-worsening storms.
"EPA" stands for _"environmental protection agency."_ To me, the name implies they should, you know, focus on protecting our environment from things like big polluters or climate change.
I wish the head of this agency would spend as much time focusing on those priorities as he does on scrubbing terms off a website.
**But it looks like Pruitt won't focus unless we force him to.[_So let Scott Pruitt and Donald Trump know that you're watching and you're ready to hold them accountable._](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f81/622b7b5b/194eefb3/307bb5f8/2258114840/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180212")**
I'm fighting to protect President Obama's legacy of progress against climate change. I hope I can count on you to stand with me.
Debbie

|
---

---

In my time growing up in York County, teaching farming techniques in rural India as part of the Peace Corps, running my family's business, and serving the people of Pennsylvania, I've seen a lot.

But let me be absolutely clear: _Nothing has shaken me to my core like the actions of President Donald Trump and his allies._

As Pennsylvania's governor, I've pledged to do everything in my power to keep President Trump's disastrous policies from coming to our commonwealth. But I can't fight him alone.

subject: Thank you, Mr. Vice President
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Conor Lamb for Congress
body:
I had to make sure you saw this news, Friend. **Joe Biden just endorsed our campaign.**

I'm honored and proud to have the support of our former Vice President. Joe Biden knows the hard-working men and women of our state -- it's why he was known as Pennsylvania's "third Senator."

He's always fought for working families, and I'm humbled he has put his faith in me to carry on that fight in Congress.

**[So please, take a few minutes to read his endorsement, then chip in to help send me to Congress so I can be a voice for PA-18's working families.](https://act.myngp.com/el/maNMDnWaECOg_Hl1_vpNwA==/LUpVfUHQ6905v7t-iAZrtg==?utm_campaign=em18_180212_Biden1_fnd_xq___KM&utm_medium=email&utm_source=&ms=em18_180212_Biden1_fnd_xq___KM&refcode=em18_180212_Biden1_fnd_xq___KM)**

Thank you,

Conor

| [**DONATE**](https://act.myngp.com/el/maNMDnWaECOg_Hl1_vpNwA==/LUpVfUHQ6905v7t-iAZrtg==?utm_campaign=em18_180212_Biden1_fnd_xq___KM&utm_medium=email&utm_source=&ms=em18_180212_Biden1_fnd_xq___KM&refcode=em18_180212_Biden1_fnd_xq___KM)
---

\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Joe Biden
Date: Thurs, Feb 8, 2018 at 2:29 PM
Subject: Conor Lamb

Folks -

"Service is the rent we pay for living."

It's a powerful notion. And one that Conor Lamb embodies.

A Marine Corps officer. A federal prosecutor. Conor has committed himself to public service.

**[Now Conor is running in Pennsylvania's 18th Congressional District and I'm hoping you'll join me in supporting him and making a donation here to his campaign for Congress.](https://act.myngp.com/el/maNMDnWaECOg_Hl1_vpNwA==/LUpVfUHQ6905v7t-iAZrtg==?utm_campaign=em18_180212_Biden1_fnd_xq___KM&utm_medium=email&utm_source=&ms=em18_180212_Biden1_fnd_xq___KM&refcode=em18_180212_Biden1_fnd_xq___KM)**

The 18th is in western Pennsylvania. It's a place where, as Conor says, "People have always gone to work, often at an early age, and often with their hands. It's where our parents and grandparents built the bridges and roads that built this country. And then built the businesses that had trucks drive over them."

Conor Lamb will be a strong voice for working people. He's not afraid to say the word "union" \-- and more importantly, he's not afraid to stand up for organized labor.

Conor knows that teachers and nurses and cops and construction workers deserve someone who will stand up for them in Congress. But that's not enough. They deserve someone who respects them, values what they do, and thanks them for the service they give every day to their community, their state, and this nation, too.

**[I hope you'll join me in supporting Conor Lamb and making a donation here to his campaign for Congress.](https://act.myngp.com/el/maNMDnWaECOg_Hl1_vpNwA==/LUpVfUHQ6905v7t-iAZrtg==?utm_campaign=em18_180212_Biden1_fnd_xq___KM&utm_medium=email&utm_source=&ms=em18_180212_Biden1_fnd_xq___KM&refcode=em18_180212_Biden1_fnd_xq___KM)**

Thanks.

\- Joe

name: Roy Cooper
subject: End gerrymandering now
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
Republican politicians have been choosing their constituents instead of constituents choosing their politicians.

|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12036fc/2fde7c66/47badd1c/1569ec46/1396219596/VEsH/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/end-partisan-gerrymandering/cooper/")
---
Something's not right here, friend.

Republican politicians have been choosing their constituents instead of constituents choosing their politicians.

Using new map-making software, Republican leaders in the North Carolina legislature and all across the country have rigged the system.

It's time to end this.

The majority of Americans want more balance in their legislatures and in Congress so more consensus can be built and more bad ideas stopped. Folks are standing up across North Carolina -- and across the country -- to demand an end to this rigged system.

[**I 'm standing with Pennsylvania Governor Tom Wolf, Missouri Senator Claire McCaskill, and a coalition of leaders and organizations across the country to restore our democracy and end gerrymandering. Will you join us?**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12036fc/2fde7c66/47badd1c/1569ec46/1396219596/VEsE/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/end-partisan-gerrymandering/cooper/")

North Carolina is a purple state. We're split pretty evenly down the middle. But that's not how we're represented.

Using technologically diabolical gerrymandering, Republicans have drawn maps that give them supermajorities in both the State House and the State Senate.

Not only that, there are 10 Republican members of Congress representing North Carolina compared to just three who are Democrats.

That just doesn't add up -- and it's not just happening here. In states across the country, Republicans have created wildly contorted districts to gain victory for their party, regardless of what voters want.

[**If you're fed up with gerrymandering, add your name to the grassroots movement that's restoring our democracy.**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12036fc/2fde7c66/47badd1c/1569ec46/1396219596/VEsF/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/end-partisan-gerrymandering/cooper/")

Thanks for all you continue to do,

Roy | | [**Add Your Name ->**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12036fc/2fde7c66/47badd1c/1569ec46/1396219596/VEsC/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/end-partisan-gerrymandering/cooper/")

name: staff@maggiehassan.com
subject: Recommended Read: Gateway to Careers Act Op-Ed from Sen. Hassan
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hello,
For the fourth year in a row, New Hampshire was ranked the strongest state in the nation based on more than a dozen measures of the economy, public safety, and quality of life.
That sustained ranking is reflective of Senator Hassan's ongoing commitment to increasing opportunities for hard-working families -- she made it a top priority as Governor, and has continued to do so as Senator.
Last week, she penned a timely Op-Ed for New Hampshire's _Union Leader_ outlining new legislation she introduced to support those who are un- or underemployed by strengthening job training programs and removing hurdles that prevent people from completing degree or credential programs and getting ahead in the workforce.
Here's an excerpt:
_In today 's economy, roughly 80 percent of jobs in the United States require some type of postsecondary education or training. As the economy changes rapidly, individuals may not have the skills and supports they need to enter -- and remain -- in the workforce. In a country where everyone who works hard should have the opportunity to succeed, we need to make sure that people are equipped with the skills and supports they need to thrive in our changing economy._
_That 's why I am introducing the Gateway to Careers Act to help expand economic opportunity for all of our people by helping strengthen our workforce so that our innovative businesses have the workers they need to continue to grow. This act would support career pathway strategies, which combine work, education, and support services, to help individuals earn industry-recognized credentials, and get a leg up on their futures._
_The bill would provide grants to support partnerships like those between community colleges and workforce development organizations or business associations. These partnerships would help individuals who are unemployed or underemployed get the job training they need to enter the workforce or move into higher-paying jobs._
[**We hope that you 'll take five minutes and read the Op-Ed in its entirety.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdfc7/2a380d31/77e323f/f1d338/3561362507/VEsH/ "http://www.unionleader.com/Another-View-Sen-Maggie-Hassan-Retraining-programs-can-provide-gateways-to-new-careers-02082018")
Senator Hassan has long been a fighter for ensuring that everyone has a fair shot at getting ahead and staying ahead, and all of us on Team Maggie stand with her in the fight for a forward-looking approach to ensuring that Americans are equipped to succeed in our changing economy.
Thanks for reading,
Team Maggie

Peter,

Can you **[follow Tom Wolf on Twitter](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf7b/73a2bc12/31389a68/1fcc6595/1712829444/VEsH/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/Twitter?utm_campaign=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td&utm_content=1+-+follow+Tom+Wolf+on+Twitter")** and **[like us on Facebook?](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf7b/73a2bc12/31389a68/1fcc6594/1712829444/VEsE/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/Facebook?utm_campaign=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td&utm_content=2+-+like+us+on+Facebook")** | | [**FOLLOW TOM ON TWITTER >>**](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf7b/73a2bc12/31389a68/1fcc659b/1712829444/VEsF/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/Twitter?utm_campaign=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td&utm_content=3+-+FOLLOW+TOM+ON+TWITTERnbspgtgt")
---

| [**LIKE TOM ON FACEBOOK >>**](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf7b/73a2bc12/31389a68/1fcc659a/1712829444/VEsC/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/Facebook?utm_campaign=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td&utm_content=4+-+LIKE+TOM+ON+FACEBOOKnbspgtgt")
---
That's it, no catch! We just want to communicate with you to show you what Governor Wolf is doing in Pennsylvania with your support.

Governor Wolf has been defending Pennsylvania against Donald Trump's toxic agenda, and he's fighting for our progressive values. He has:
* Expanded Medicaid to provide healthcare for 720,000 more people,
* Reauthorized CHIP funding and expanded coverage for children by 20 percent,
* Restored the Corbett education cuts and increased the number of children in Pre-K and full-day kindergarten,
* And stopped the most anti-choice bill in the country.

We've accomplished so much with Governor Wolf, but we have a lot of work ahead of us in 2018. If we're going to win this November, we need grassroots support across the country -- so we want to make sure you're up to date on how to be involved!

**So take just a few seconds to follow us on Twitter and like us on Facebook by clicking the links below:**

**[Follow us on Twitter >>](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf7b/73a2bc12/31389a68/1fcc6599/1712829444/VEsD/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/Twitter?utm_campaign=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td&utm_content=5+-+Follow+us+on+Twitternbspgtgt")**

**[Like us on Facebook >>](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf7b/73a2bc12/31389a68/1fcc6598/1712829444/VEsA/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/Facebook?utm_campaign=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td&utm_content=6+-+Like+us+on+Facebooknbspgtgt")**

Thanks,

Tori

Tori DeGiosio
Digital Director
Tom Wolf for Governor

Paid for by Tom Wolf for Governor
Contributions or gifts to Tom Wolf for Governor are not tax deductible.

This email was sent to: **dpwillis67@gmail.com**
If that is not your preferred email address, you can update your information [here](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf7b/73a2bc12/31389a68/1fcc659f/1712829444/VEsB/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://action.wolfforpa.com/changeaddress?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_medium=email&utm_source=tomwolf&utm_content=7+-+here&utm_campaign=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td&source=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td")
We believe that emails are an important way for the campaign to stay in contact with supporters like you. This campaign appreciates any feedback you might have -- positive or negative. [Click here](mailto:info@wolfforpa.com?Subject=Feedback) to contact the campaign with any questions, concerns, or ideas. [Click here](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecf7b/73a2bc12/31389a68/1fcc659e/1712829444/VEsO/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_medium=email&utm_source=tomwolf&utm_content=8+-+Click+here&utm_campaign=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td&source=em18_180203_social_engg_clq_intro__td") if you would like to unsubscribe from Tom Wolf for Governor email updates.
